title: memecylon normandii
intro: Memecylon normandii, also known as the wild plum, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Sri Lanka. It is a medium-sized evergreen tree that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's bark and leaves are used in herbal preparations to treat various health conditions, such as fever, diarrhea, and skin infections, and have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving digestive health. Herbal preparations of Memecylon normandii include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
